title: quercus aliena subsp. griffithii
intro: Quercus aliena subsp. griffithii, also known as the Himalayan white oak, is a subspecies of deciduous oak native to the Himalayan region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing cholesterol levels, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against certain types of cancer. In traditional herbal medicine, Quercus aliena subsp. griffithii is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and its extracts are also used in modern pharmaceuticals.
